{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Senior Civil Engineer",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Senior Civil Engineer

Job Location: Obajana Plant, Kogi

Job Summary


	The Senior Civil Enginer at Dangote Cement Plc is responsible for ensuring safe, reliable, and efficient co-operation and support across departments, executing inspections, troubleshooting breakdowns, and maintaining high safety and performance standards.


Key Duties and Responbilities


	Plan and execute civil engineering jobs across departments.
	Design, carry out structural analyses and detailed plans by using AutoCAD or Civil 3D.
	Planning, design and construction of internal roads and drainage systems.
	carry out vendor development and sourcing.
	Enforce zoning laws, environmental regulations and local building codes.
	Maintain daily maintenance logs and reports.
	Record equipment history and failure data.
	Update maintenance checklists and work orders.
	Suggest improvements for equipment reliability and efficiency.
	Reduce downtime and maintenance costs.
	Implement best maintenance practices and new technologies.
	Ensure strict compliance with safety procedures and company policies.
	Provide on-the-job training and skill development to team members.


Requirements


	A Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree or its equivalent in Civil Engineering or a related discipline.
	A minimum of nine (9) years of work experience as a Civil Engineer.
	Advanced proficiency in Civil Engineering design software, cost estimation and structural analysis .
	Good troubleshooting and analytical skills.
	Leadership and team management ability.
	Excellent knowledge of safety standards and procedures.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-21",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Senior Civil Engineer Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Senior Civil Engineer

Job Location: Obajana Plant, Kogi

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Senior Civil Enginer at Dangote Cement Plc is responsible for ensuring safe, reliable, and efficient co-operation and support across departments, executing inspections, troubleshooting breakdowns, and maintaining high safety and performance standards.


Key Duties and Responbilities


	Plan and execute civil engineering jobs across departments.
	Design, carry out structural analyses and detailed plans by using AutoCAD or Civil 3D.
	Planning, design and construction of internal roads and drainage systems.
	carry out vendor development and sourcing.
	Enforce zoning laws, environmental regulations and local building codes.
	Maintain daily maintenance logs and reports.
	Record equipment history and failure data.
	Update maintenance checklists and work orders.
	Suggest improvements for equipment reliability and efficiency.
	Reduce downtime and maintenance costs.
	Implement best maintenance practices and new technologies.
	Ensure strict compliance with safety procedures and company policies.
	Provide on-the-job training and skill development to team members.


Requirements


	A Bachelor’s Degree or its equivalent in Civil Engineering or a related discipline.
	A minimum of nine (9) years of work experience as a Civil Engineer.
	Advanced proficiency in Civil Engineering design software, cost estimation and structural analysis .
	Good troubleshooting and analytical skills.
	Leadership and team management ability.
	Excellent knowledge of safety standards and procedures.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Assistant General Manager, Mechanical (Hydraulics Specialist)",
  "description": "Dangote Group is one of Nigeria&rsquo;s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard &ndash; earned reputation for excellent business practices and products&rsquo; quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant General Manager, Mechanical (Hydraulics Specialist)

Job Location: Obajana Plant, Kogi

Job Summary


	A Hydraulic Specialist is responsible for Operation, Maintenance, Trouble Shooting of hydraulic systems for FLS Coolers, IKN Coolers, Loesche VRM and OK Mills.
	They utilize their expertise in fluid mechanics, physics, and engineering principles to analyze and develop sustainable solutions for bulk material handling equipment (Stacker and Reclaimer), kilns clinker Cooler hydraulic systems.
	This role plays a crucial part in the planning and implementation of various hydraulic engineering projects, ensuring efficiency, safety, and environmental sustainability.
	Exposure of Cement Plant Equipment Stacker Reclaimers, Rawmills, Kilns Coolers and Cement mill General Maintenance.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Operation and Maintenance, Trouble shooting of &nbsp;hydraulic system Problems, including, Stacker luffing/hydraulic cylinders, hydraulic hose, directional control valves and proportionate valve, Cooler Hydraulic Drives, VRM Hydraulic System.
	Conducting feasibility studies, including trouble shooting and assessments, to determine the viability and potential impacts of hydraulic faults.
	Collaborating with other engineers and professionals to develop comprehensive hydraulic designs and engineering plans in ensuring equipment reliability and optimization.
	Identifying and evaluating potential risks and hazards associated with hydraulic systems, and implementing appropriate safety measures.
	Performing hydraulic calculations, simulations, and modelling to predict the behaviour and performance of equipment&rsquo;s.
	Overseeing the construction and installation of hydraulic systems, ensuring compliance with design specifications and industry standards.
	Conducting inspections and assessments of existing hydraulic infrastructure, identifying maintenance or repair needs, and providing recommendations.
	Conducting research and staying updated on the latest advancements in hydraulic engineering technologies and methodologies.
	Managing budgets, timelines, and resources effectively to ensure plant success.
	Collaborating with clients, stakeholders, and regulatory bodies to ensure compliance with environmental regulations and permitting requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s Degree in Mechanical Engineering, with Hydraulics Training/Hydraulics Special Course &nbsp;or a related field. A Master&#39;s degree is preferred.
	Professional Engineer (P.E.) license is preferred.
	Above 15 years of experience in cement plant hydraulics Operation, maintenance and Trouble Shooting.
	Proven experience in hydraulic engineering, preferably in the design and maintenance of hydraulic systems.
	Knowledge of study of Hydraulic Circuits /hydraulic Notations and Symbols relevant software and tools used in hydraulic engineering, such as AutoCAD, Microsoft office and SAP Module.
	Strong mathematical and computational skills.
	Familiarity with environmental impact assessment processes and regulations.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Experience in project management and working with multidisciplinary teams.
	Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.
	Ability to&nbsp;&nbsp;work effectively under pressure and meet deadlines.


Skills and Behaviours:


	In-depth knowledge of hydraulic engineering principles and fluid mechanics.
	Proficiency in using computer-aided design (CAD) software and hydraulic modelling software.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to assess complex hydraulic systems and propose effective solutions.
	Excellent in plant management and organizational skills to handle multiple task simultaneously.
	Effective communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in designing and analyzing hydraulic systems.
	Strong knowledge of environmental regulations and sustainability principles.
	Ability to work independently and make informed decisions based on engineering principles and best practices.
	Familiarity with industry standards and codes related to hydraulic engineering.
	Ability to adapt to changing technologies and industry trends.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training &amp; Development.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-07-20",
  "validThrough": "2026-08-21",

Assistant General Manager, Mechanical (Hydraulics Specialist) Job at Dangote Group




        
    

        
        Dangote Group is one of Nigeria’s most diversified business conglomerates with a hard – earned reputation for excellent business practices and products’ quality with its operational headquarters in the bustling metropolis of Lagos, Nigeria in West Africa.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Assistant General Manager, Mechanical (Hydraulics Specialist)

Job Location: Obajana Plant, Kogi

Job Summary
     

            
    




	A Hydraulic Specialist is responsible for Operation, Maintenance, Trouble Shooting of hydraulic systems for FLS Coolers, IKN Coolers, Loesche VRM and OK Mills.
	They utilize their expertise in fluid mechanics, physics, and engineering principles to analyze and develop sustainable solutions for bulk material handling equipment (Stacker and Reclaimer), kilns clinker Cooler hydraulic systems.
	This role plays a crucial part in the planning and implementation of various hydraulic engineering projects, ensuring efficiency, safety, and environmental sustainability.
	Exposure of Cement Plant Equipment Stacker Reclaimers, Rawmills, Kilns Coolers and Cement mill General Maintenance.


Key Duties and Responsibilities


	Operation and Maintenance, Trouble shooting of  hydraulic system Problems, including, Stacker luffing/hydraulic cylinders, hydraulic hose, directional control valves and proportionate valve, Cooler Hydraulic Drives, VRM Hydraulic System.
	Conducting feasibility studies, including trouble shooting and assessments, to determine the viability and potential impacts of hydraulic faults.
	Collaborating with other engineers and professionals to develop comprehensive hydraulic designs and engineering plans in ensuring equipment reliability and optimization.
	Identifying and evaluating potential risks and hazards associated with hydraulic systems, and implementing appropriate safety measures.
	Performing hydraulic calculations, simulations, and modelling to predict the behaviour and performance of equipment’s.
	Overseeing the construction and installation of hydraulic systems, ensuring compliance with design specifications and industry standards.
	Conducting inspections and assessments of existing hydraulic infrastructure, identifying maintenance or repair needs, and providing recommendations.
	Conducting research and staying updated on the latest advancements in hydraulic engineering technologies and methodologies.
	Managing budgets, timelines, and resources effectively to ensure plant success.
	Collaborating with clients, stakeholders, and regulatory bodies to ensure compliance with environmental regulations and permitting requirements.


Requirements


	Bachelor's Degree in Mechanical Engineering, with Hydraulics Training/Hydraulics Special Course  or a related field. A Master's degree is preferred.
	Professional Engineer (P.E.) license is preferred.
	Above 15 years of experience in cement plant hydraulics Operation, maintenance and Trouble Shooting.
	Proven experience in hydraulic engineering, preferably in the design and maintenance of hydraulic systems.
	Knowledge of study of Hydraulic Circuits /hydraulic Notations and Symbols relevant software and tools used in hydraulic engineering, such as AutoCAD, Microsoft office and SAP Module.
	Strong mathematical and computational skills.
	Familiarity with environmental impact assessment processes and regulations.
	Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
	Experience in project management and working with multidisciplinary teams.
	Strong problem-solving and critical-thinking abilities.
	Ability to  work effectively under pressure and meet deadlines.


Skills and Behaviours:


	In-depth knowledge of hydraulic engineering principles and fluid mechanics.
	Proficiency in using computer-aided design (CAD) software and hydraulic modelling software.
	Strong analytical and problem-solving skills to assess complex hydraulic systems and propose effective solutions.
	Excellent in plant management and organizational skills to handle multiple task simultaneously.
	Effective communication and teamwork skills to collaborate with clients, colleagues, and stakeholders.
	Attention to detail and accuracy in designing and analyzing hydraulic systems.
	Strong knowledge of environmental regulations and sustainability principles.
	Ability to work independently and make informed decisions based on engineering principles and best practices.
	Familiarity with industry standards and codes related to hydraulic engineering.
	Ability to adapt to changing technologies and industry trends.


Benefits


	Private Health Insurance
	Paid Time Off
	Training & Development.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

  "description": "Kennedia Consulting Limited (KCL) - We provide HR consulting, HR strategy, workforce planning, culture and HR transformation, recruitment and talent acquisition, assessment and development centres.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Head of Compliance &amp; Internal Control
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time&nbsp;

Job Summary


	The Head of Compliance &amp; Internal Control will oversee the Bank&rsquo;s compliance and internal control framework, ensuring adherence to all regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry best practices.
	The role is responsible for identifying and mitigating compliance risks, strengthening internal controls, supporting regulatory examinations, and promoting a strong culture of ethics, governance, and risk management across the Bank.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and monitor the Bank&rsquo;s compliance and internal control framework.
	Ensure compliance with regulations issued by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), NDIC, EFCC, NFIU, and other relevant regulatory bodies.
	Conduct periodic compliance reviews, internal control assessments, and operational audits.
	Identify control weaknesses, recommend corrective actions, and monitor implementation.
	Develop and enforce policies, procedures, and internal control standards.
	Monitor Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Counter-Terrorism Financing (CTF), and Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance.
	Prepare and submit regulatory reports accurately and within stipulated timelines.
	Coordinate regulatory examinations and internal/external audit activities.
	Investigate compliance breaches and operational incidents, recommending appropriate remedial actions.
	Provide compliance training and advisory support to business units and promote a culture of regulatory compliance.
	Report compliance and internal control performance to Executive Management and the Board.


Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Law, Business Administration, or a related field. A Master&rsquo;s degree is an added advantage.
	Professional certifications such as ACAMS, ACA, ACCA, CISA, CIA, CRCMP, or other relevant certifications are highly desirable.
	Minimum of 7&ndash;10 years of experience in Compliance, Internal Control, Internal Audit, Risk Management, or Regulatory Compliance within the banking or financial services industry, with at least 3 years in a leadership role.
	Strong knowledge of CBN regulations, AML/CFT requirements, KYC, corporate governance, and risk management practices.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, communication, and leadership skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and sound professional judgment.


Salary
N300,000 - N450,000 Monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: adeniyi_kennedia@outlook.com using the Job Position as the subject of the email.&nbsp;",

Head of Compliance & Internal Control Job at Kennedia Consulting Limited




        
    

        
        Kennedia Consulting Limited (KCL) - We provide HR consulting, HR strategy, workforce planning, culture and HR transformation, recruitment and talent acquisition, assessment and development centres.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Head of Compliance & Internal Control
Job Location: Lekki, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time 

Job Summary
     

            
    




	The Head of Compliance & Internal Control will oversee the Bank’s compliance and internal control framework, ensuring adherence to all regulatory requirements, internal policies, and industry best practices.
	The role is responsible for identifying and mitigating compliance risks, strengthening internal controls, supporting regulatory examinations, and promoting a strong culture of ethics, governance, and risk management across the Bank.


Key Responsibilities


	Develop, implement, and monitor the Bank’s compliance and internal control framework.
	Ensure compliance with regulations issued by the Central Bank of Nigeria (CBN), NDIC, EFCC, NFIU, and other relevant regulatory bodies.
	Conduct periodic compliance reviews, internal control assessments, and operational audits.
	Identify control weaknesses, recommend corrective actions, and monitor implementation.
	Develop and enforce policies, procedures, and internal control standards.
	Monitor Anti-Money Laundering (AML), Counter-Terrorism Financing (CTF), and Know Your Customer (KYC) compliance.
	Prepare and submit regulatory reports accurately and within stipulated timelines.
	Coordinate regulatory examinations and internal/external audit activities.
	Investigate compliance breaches and operational incidents, recommending appropriate remedial actions.
	Provide compliance training and advisory support to business units and promote a culture of regulatory compliance.
	Report compliance and internal control performance to Executive Management and the Board.


Requirements


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, Law, Business Administration, or a related field. A Master’s degree is an added advantage.
	Professional certifications such as ACAMS, ACA, ACCA, CISA, CIA, CRCMP, or other relevant certifications are highly desirable.
	Minimum of 7–10 years of experience in Compliance, Internal Control, Internal Audit, Risk Management, or Regulatory Compliance within the banking or financial services industry, with at least 3 years in a leadership role.
	Strong knowledge of CBN regulations, AML/CFT requirements, KYC, corporate governance, and risk management practices.
	Excellent analytical, investigative, communication, and leadership skills.
	High level of integrity, confidentiality, and sound professional judgment.


Salary
N300,000 - N450,000 Monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their updated CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.     

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Marketer",
  "description": "Elizabeth Maddeux provides a range of human resources solutions designed to empower your workforce, enhance technical proficiency, refine soft skills, and accelerate organizational growth for better impacts by enhancing organizational dynamics through a well-rounded and robust human resource intervention.

We are&nbsp;&nbsp;recruiting to fill the position below:

&nbsp;Job Position: Marketer&nbsp;

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos (Hybrid &ndash; 4 Days Off-site, 1 Day On-site)
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Real Estate

Summary


	We are seeking a results-driven and proactive Real Estate Marketer to drive property sales, generate quality leads, and build lasting client relationships.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of the real estate market and proven ability to meet sales targets through effective marketing and&nbsp;&nbsp;business development strategies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities to generate property sales and investment leads.
	Market and promote the company&#39;s real estate properties across various channels.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective buyers, investors, and corporate clients.
	Conduct property inspections and presentations for potential clients.
	Understand clients&#39; property needs and recommend suitable real estate solutions.
	Develop and implement effective sales and marketing strategies to increase property sales.
	Maintain an active sales pipeline and provide regular reports on sales and marketing activities.
	Negotiate property deals and support clients throughout the sales process.
	Achieve and exceed assigned monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Stay informed about market trends, competitor activities, and property developments.


Qualification &amp; Requirements


	Bachelor&rsquo;s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	2&ndash;4 years of experience in real estate sales, marketing, or business development.
	Proven track record of achieving sales targets in the real estate industry.
	Strong client acquisition, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with CRM tools is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of the Lagos real estate market is highly desirable.


Salary
N300,000 monthly (Plus 10% Commission)

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: careers@elizabethmaddeux.com using &quot;Marketer&quot; as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.",

Marketer Job at Elizabeth Maddeux




        
    

        
        Elizabeth Maddeux provides a range of human resources solutions designed to empower your workforce, enhance technical proficiency, refine soft skills, and accelerate organizational growth for better impacts by enhancing organizational dynamics through a well-rounded and robust human resource intervention.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Marketer 

Job Location: Lekki, Lagos (Hybrid – 4 Days Off-site, 1 Day On-site)
Employment Type: Full-time
Industry: Real Estate

Summary
     

            
    




	We are seeking a results-driven and proactive Real Estate Marketer to drive property sales, generate quality leads, and build lasting client relationships.
	The ideal candidate will have a strong understanding of the real estate market and proven ability to meet sales targets through effective marketing and  business development strategies.


Key Responsibilities


	Identify and pursue new business opportunities to generate property sales and investment leads.
	Market and promote the company's real estate properties across various channels.
	Build and maintain strong relationships with prospective buyers, investors, and corporate clients.
	Conduct property inspections and presentations for potential clients.
	Understand clients' property needs and recommend suitable real estate solutions.
	Develop and implement effective sales and marketing strategies to increase property sales.
	Maintain an active sales pipeline and provide regular reports on sales and marketing activities.
	Negotiate property deals and support clients throughout the sales process.
	Achieve and exceed assigned monthly and quarterly sales targets.
	Stay informed about market trends, competitor activities, and property developments.


Qualification & Requirements


	Bachelor’s degree in Marketing, Business Administration, Estate Management, Mass Communication, or a related field.
	2–4 years of experience in real estate sales, marketing, or business development.
	Proven track record of achieving sales targets in the real estate industry.
	Strong client acquisition, negotiation, and relationship management skills.
	Excellent communication, presentation, and interpersonal skills.
	Proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite; experience with CRM tools is an added advantage.
	Knowledge of the Lagos real estate market is highly desirable.


Salary
N300,000 monthly (Plus 10% Commission)

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using "Marketer" as the subject of the email.

Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.    

    

        Click here to Apply

Medical Administrative Assistant Job at Uprightmarket




        
    

        
        Uprightmarket - The company operates an investment and training platform that provides accredited investors with access to alternative investment opportunities beyond traditional stocks, real estate , commodities and bonds. These include: hedge funds, private equity, real estate funds, and digital assets.

We are  recruiting to fill the position below:

 Job Position: Medical Administrative Assistant

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos 
Employment Type: Full-time

Summary
     

            
    




	We are looking for a Medical administrative assistant to organize our medical facility’s functions.
	You will support day-to-day operations by performing administrative tasks and ensuring high quality customer service for our patients.


Responsibilities


	Interview patients for case histories prior to appointments
	Update and maintain patients’ health records
	Assist patients with initial paperwork
	Schedule and coordinate appointments
	Process insurance claims in compliance with law requirements
	Use medical software to support all transactions
	Manage receivable and payable accounts and maintain financial records
	Answer patients’ queries and ensure quality customer service
	Collaborate with doctors and nurses to help with medical examinations, schedule tests and order supplies
	Ensure compliance with procedures
	Keep up-to-date with changes in medical and insurance legislation.


Requirements and Skills


	Hands on experience with medical software and MS Office
	Knowledge of healthcare operations
	Familiarity with medical and insurance legislation
	Excellent organizational and multitasking skills
	Customer-oriented communication skills.


Salary
N140,000 - N230,000 monthly.

Method of Application
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply
